perturbation.original_text: The Parties shall, as soon as practicable but not later than within ninety (90) days after the Effective Date, form a joint advisory committee (the "Joint Advisory Committee" or "JAC"). The JAC will provide a forum for the good faith discussion of major matters related to this Agreement, including in particular (but not limited to) matters of commercial performance, supply, overall performance, capital investment and business planning (strategy and management), and the transition to Customer-Supplied Materials arrangements contemplated by Section 12.1(f), but also any other items, matters or activities, including with respect to any Facility.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties shall, as soon as practicable but not later than within ninety (90) days after the Effective Date, form a joint advisory committee (the "Joint Advisory Committee" or "JAC").  The JAC will address matters of publicity, but also any other items, matters or activities, including with respect to any Facility.
perturbation.explanation: Moving the formation of the Joint Advisory Committee to deal with publicity, instead of issues of commercial performance, supply, overall performance, capital investment and business planning obscures its intended purpose and potential effectiveness. The original language establishes a committee to support important business matters for the contract, which this change undermines.
perturbation.law_explanation: The change obscures the intended function of the Joint Advisory Committee, moving it from a committee that would handle manufacturing/supply issues, into a committee dealing with publicity. Because 21 CFR § 211.194 speaks to the need for written procedures for investigating discrepancies and failures of a batch or any of its components to meet the specifications, this section is contradicted because the movement of the JAC's function means that the Joint Advisory Committee may not function to address quality or manufacturing control issues.

perturbation.justification: The original contract text establishes a committee to address critical business and operational matters, including commercial performance, supply, capital investment, and manufacturing considerations. The changed text shifts the JAC's focus to publicity, potentially neglecting the oversight of discrepancies and failures in manufacturing processes. The Law Explanation states that 21 CFR § 211.194 is contradicted because the JAC may not function to address quality or manufacturing control issues. Although the snippets provided do not contain the body of 21 CFR § 211.194, the Law Explanation's claim regarding the contradiction of this section, along with the Perturbation Explanation, suggests that the changed text impairs the ability of the JAC to address matters of manufacturing quality control which would be handled by a Joint Advisory Committee under the original language. Given that this type of change aligns with the Structural Flaws - Legal Contradiction definition of a contract change which undermines the intended purpose and effectiveness of a legally required committee, this means a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: Manufacturer hereby covenants to Customer that: (a) The Facility and all equipment, tooling and molds utilized in the manufacture and supply of Product hereunder by or on behalf of Manufacturer shall, during the Term of this Agreement, be maintained in good operating condition and shall be maintained and operated in accordance with all applicable Laws. The manufacturing and storage operations, procedures and processes utilized in manufacture and supply of Product hereunder (including the Facility) shall be in full compliance with all applicable Laws, including cGMP and health and safety laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Manufacturer hereby covenants to Customer that: (a) The Facility will be decorated in a fashion fitting of the aesthetic expectations of Manufacturer leadership. The manufacturing and storage operations, procedures and processes utilized in manufacture and supply of Product hereunder (including the Facility) shall be in full compliance with all applicable Laws, including cGMP and health and safety laws.
perturbation.explanation: The change recontextualizes maintenance commitments as a matter of aesthetic preference. The original text commits the Manufacturer to operating conditions and legal requirements. The new version places aesthetics before maintenance requirements.
perturbation.law_explanation: The regulatory code 21 CFR § 211.25 directly addresses the need for written procedures assigning responsibility for sanitation and waste disposal. By changing the text and focusing on cosmetic appearance, this section is contradicted because the cosmetic focus means that no measures are directly being taken to address proper handling of materials.

perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a requirement to maintain equipment in good operating condition and in accordance with applicable laws with a requirement to decorate the facility in a fashion fitting the aesthetic expectations of Manufacturer leadership. This directly contradicts the original text's commitment to compliance with applicable laws including cGMP (Current Good Manufacturing Practice) by prioritizing aesthetics over maintenance and legal compliance.

perturbation.original_text: Customer may exercise its inspection rights under this Section 5.3(d) upon receipt of any information that would suggest to a reasonable Person that Manufacturer is not fulfilling its obligations under this Section 5.3.
perturbation.changed_text: Customer may exercise its inspection rights under this Section 5.3(d) upon receipt of permission from Manufacturer Legal that Manufacturer is not fulfilling its obligations under this Section 5.3.
perturbation.explanation: By requiring permission from Manufacturer Legal, this imposes a new requirement on the original obligation to be able to inspect, in effect creating a delay or complete impediment to checking for non-compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: 29 CFR § 516.2 concerns the need for records in relation to wage practices. The changes made contradict that law, because, with the introduction of Manufacturer Legal's permission, the ability of the inspectors to inspect those records can be effectively impeded, should permission be delayed or denied.

perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a requirement that the Customer obtain permission from Manufacturer Legal before exercising its inspection rights. This contradicts the original text, which allowed inspection upon receipt of information suggesting non-compliance. The Law Explanation states that this change impedes the ability to inspect records, potentially violating 29 CFR § 516.2. While Scraped Law Snippet 1 is incomplete, the Law Explanation strongly suggests the change could impede inspection and thus create a contradiction, because permission could be delayed or denied.
